zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel of Cambridge於大約1409年出生在英國越可爾康尼斯布盧 卡素時，她的父親Richard of Conisburgh 3rd Earl of Cambridge25歲，母親Anne de Mortimer Countess of Cambridge22歲。她於大約1422年與Sir Thomas Grey結婚。她去世於1484年10月2日在英國艾可蕾托 伊斯頓享年76歲。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Cambridge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Antrim and Cork): shortened Anglicized form of Mac Ambróis, see McCambridge .

English: habitational name from Cambridge, the university city and county town of Cambridgeshire in East Anglia. Until the late 14th century this place was known as Grantabrycge or Cantebrigge ‘bridge on the (river) Granta’, from a Celtic river name meaning ‘marshy river’. Under Norman influence Granta- became Cam-.

English: habitational name from the village of Cambridge near Slimbridge in Gloucestershire. This place is early-recorded as Cambrigga, and named for the Cam, a Celtic river name meaning ‘crooked, winding’.

variants.name: McCambridge
meanings: Irish (northern) and Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Ambróis ‘son of Ambrose ’. This name has been established in the Glens of Antrim, Northern Ireland, since the 18th century.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel of Cambridge was born about 1409, in Conisbrough Castle, Yorkshire, England, her father, Richard of Conisburgh 3rd Earl of Cambridge, was 25 and her mother, Anne de Mortimer Countess of Cambridge, was 22. She married Sir Thomas Grey about 1422. She died on 2 October 1484, in Little Easton, Essex, England, at the age of 76.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel of Cambridge est née environ 1409, à Conisbrough Castle, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Richard of Conisburgh 3rd Earl of Cambridge, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Anne de Mortimer Countess of Cambridge, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Grey environ 1422. Elle est décédée le 2 octobre 1484, à Little Easton, Essex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 76 ans.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel of Cambridge è nata il circa 1409 nel Conisbrough Castle, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Richard of Conisburgh 3rd Earl of Cambridge, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Anne de Mortimer Countess of Cambridge, aveva 22 anni. ja.lifeSummary: Isabel of Cambridgeが約1409年にイギリスヨークシュアイアコニスブロウ カストルで生まれた時，父Richard of Conisburgh 3rd Earl of Cambridgeは25歳，母Anne de Mortimer Countess of Cambridgeは22歳であった。約1422年にSir Thomas Greyと結婚した。彼女は1484年10月2日にイギリスエセックスリットル エアストンで76歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel of Cambridge(이)가 대략 1409년 영국 요르크실르 코니스브루그h 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard of Conisburgh 3rd Earl of Cambridge(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne de Mortimer Countess of Cambridge(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 대략 1422년 Sir Thomas Grey(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1484년 10월 2일 영국 에세스 리틀 이스트에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel of Cambridge родилась около 1409 г. в местности конисброую кастл, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard of Conisburgh 3rd Earl of Cambridge было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne de Mortimer Countess of Cambridge, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Grey около 1422 г. Она умерла 2 октября 1484 г. в местности литл эастон, эссекс, Англия в возрасте 76 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel of Cambridge nació aproximadamente 1409, en Conisbrough Castle, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard of Conisburgh 3rd Earl of Cambridge, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Anne de Mortimer Countess of Cambridge, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Sir Thomas Grey aproximadamente 1422. Murió el 2 de octubre de 1484, en Little Easton, Essex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 76 años.
